title: Mitigation: Reducing Our Footprint
text:
[cite_start]Goal: To achieve carbon neutrality in all facilities and operations by 2030.

Essential Audits

Energy Audit
Water Audit
Waste Audit



Easy Wins (Under $500)

[cite_start]Lighting: Replace all bulbs with LEDs.
[cite_start]Leak Detection: Test toilets every 6 months.
[cite_start]Hospitality: Use ceramic mugs instead of plastic.

title: From Vision to Action
text:
Building on the foundation laid by the 2022–2024 Creation Care Task Force, a new team was commissioned in September 2025 to turn recommendations into reality. Our mission is to provide congregations with practical tools and theological grounding needed to lead in a changing world.
Preparation is about more than just technical steps. We invite you to explore these real-world stories from throughout our diocese, highlighting how others are putting resilience into practice, allowing us to better relate to our fellow travelers and their experiences. Additionally, if you have a story to share, please contact us. We want to help spread stories of both successes and failures to all our congregations.
We focus our efforts in three vital areas: Mitigation, Adaptation, and Formation.


Lowering our impact. Mitigation is about slowing the progress of climate change by shrinking our environmental footprint. This includes practical steps to lower energy consumption, conserve water, and move toward zero-waste communities. | Goal: To be better stewards of the resources we use daily.



Building resilience. While we work to slow climate change, we must also prepare for its effects. Adaptation involves taking proactive measures to reduce our vulnerability to climate-related challenges and ensuring our congregations remain safe, prepared, and resilient. | Goal: To protect our people and our properties in a shifting climate.



Re-imagining our relationship with Earth. Meaningful change involves emotion, thought, and action. We seek to engage all three as our community consciously considers our relationship with all of God’s Creation, moving away from a mindset of domination and toward acts of loving and responsible stewardship. | Goal: To honor the Creator by loving and protecting every aspect of God’s creation.

Resource Links
Zero Waste Church: A North Carolina Episcopal Church with a website filled with potential projects for reducing waste. Keep Texas Beautiful: An organization dedicated to litter cleanup, with guides for group activities and other resources.
For more information, contact Alan Jackson and Robin Bullington.
